text.title: ETL Migration Engine
text.summary: Python ETL skeleton for PM tool migration with dry-run flag, per-wave checkpointing, and ID mapping output.

rule.statement: Design the migration engine as a CLI tool the agent invokes; do not run migrations as inline LLM calls.
rule.rationale: Long-running migrations exceed agent token budgets; the CLI pattern keeps the agent as orchestrator with human-reviewable artifacts at each checkpoint.

rule.statement: Run a full dry-run on production data into a sandbox target one week before cutover; measure exact runtime then add 50% buffer.
rule.rationale: Rate limits routinely double planned cutover downtime; dry-run measurement is the only reliable source of timing data.

example.code:
from dataclasses import dataclass from typing import Dict
@dataclass class MigrationConfig: source_tool: str target_tool: str field_mapping: Dict[str, str] # source_field -> target_field status_mapping: Dict[str, str] # source_status -> target_status priority_mapping: Dict[str, str] dry_run: bool = True # always default to dry_run=True
example.note: Always default dry_run to True to prevent accidental live migrations.

list.item: Extract: pull all issues from source; log count before processing
Transform: apply field_mapping, status_mapping, priority_mapping per record; log per-record errors without halting
Load: create issues in target; record source_id → target_id mapping; checkpoint after each successful write
Validate: compare three counts (issues, comments, attachments-by-byte) and sample 50 random items by hand

rule.statement: Freeze the field mapping specification before the first dry-run; forbid runtime mapping mutations.
rule.rationale: Each mapping change requires re-transforming already-processed records; LLMs that "fix" mappings on the fly silently corrupt the ID mapping and cause orphaned links.

example.note: The checkpoint file enables resume from any point; critical for multi-hour migrations interrupted by rate limits.

section.title: Post-Migration Link Fixing
section.p: Issue links, @mentions in comments, and commit message references all contain source-tool IDs. Fix these in a separate pass after the main migration using the ID mapping JSON.

rule.statement: Force-list the 0.5% of failed records; they are always the most-linked tickets.
rule.rationale: A 99.5% success rate looks fine in aggregate but the missing records are disproportionately high-value due to cross-reference density.

antipattern.description: Skipping @mention and user-ID resolution during comment migration.
antipattern.reason: Strips all @mentions silently, breaking notification chains and audit trails for team collaboration history.

text.reference: Checkpointed loader script — resumes from .checkpoint on rerun
